WTI.data_anomaly: fast_info prev_close $95.99 (+1.8%) vs history prev_close $93.50 (+4.5%). Using fast_info as headline; history used for technicals only.

macro_summary: Monday's session delivered a meaningful risk-on relief rally — SPY gained 0.9% to $669.03 and QQQ rose 1.17% to $600.38, with VIX falling 13.6% to 23.51, its sharpest single-day fear reduction in weeks. The recovery was catalysed partly by NVIDIA's GTC 2026 keynote and a slight easing of the worst-case Hormuz scenarios, though per the news digest, breadth conviction remains thin with mega-cap tech still lagging and the structural hawkish Fed pause narrative intact. The FOMC rate decision on Wednesday (March 19) is now the dominant near-term binary event, arriving the same day as Micron's earnings — making the next 48 hours unusually high-stakes for the watchlist.
commodities_note: WTI crude shows a data conflict: the pipeline's fast_info source shows $97.71 (+1.79% daily), while the history-based close implies a larger move from $93.50 (+4.5%). Using the fast_info figure of $97.71 as the headline number, consistent with the pipeline's guidance. The macro_news from CNBC notes oil prices jumped over 2% on Tuesday as uncertainty lingered over a US-led coalition to protect Hormuz shipping — the Hormuz crisis is now in its eighth day with vessel traffic reportedly collapsed to just 2 ships per day versus a normal 138, per the news digest. Gold slipped 0.32% to $5,014.90, a minor pullback from recent highs well above $5,000; at these levels the metal reflects persistent safe-haven demand from the conflict but no new impetus to chase higher. The 10-year Treasury yield eased slightly to 4.22%, providing marginal relief for rate-sensitive tech names, though not enough to shift the hawkish pause narrative materially.

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Upgrades to WATCH on a close above SGD 57.59 (the 50-day average) with above-average volume.

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Becomes actionable on a confirmed second close above the 50-day average of SGD 20.89 with volume pickup, or on a pullback to SGD 20.50-20.70.

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Would reconsider above SGD 37.27 (50-day average) with volume, or after the May 7 earnings print.

C6L_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to CAUTION — Singapore Airlines' 8.35% free cash flow yield and 5.35% dividend are genuinely attractive at first glance, but a 64.3% forecast earnings decline (per analyst consensus covering 14 analysts) and a forward price-to-earnings ratio of 17.3x that sits 26% above the Singapore cluster median of 13.7x make the valuation uncomfortable for a business facing structurally higher fuel and rerouting costs. Revenue growth is essentially flat at 1.9% — less than half the forward price-to-earnings ratio, a valuation concern flagged by the framework. The Hormuz crisis, now entering its second week, is the core operational headwind: per the news digest and macro_news, airspace closures have removed 16-18% of global air cargo capacity, jet fuel prices have surged with flight detours adding thousands of dollars per leg, and freight rate inflation is compounding the airline's cost base. At SGD 6.57, the stock sits just below its 50-day average of SGD 6.63 (RSI at 42.8, neutral-bearish) with support at SGD 6.34; a break there opens a path toward SGD 5.57. The signal would reconsider if the Hormuz situation de-escalates and fuel cost guidance stabilises, or on a confirmed close above SGD 6.65 resistance with volume.

Z74_SI.entry_block: BLOCKED: +7.8% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: RSI 62 (>=55).
Z74_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Signal overridden from raw CAUTION to CAUTION (confirmed) — entry is hard-blocked at 7.8% above the 50-day average of SGD 4.76, with RSI at 61.8 exceeding the 55 threshold required for a trend exception. The Zscaler memorandum of understanding with Singtel (reported March 15 by Insider Monkey) for zero-trust security across Southeast Asia confirms the structural cybersecurity narrative, and the 30-consecutive-sessions condition for a trend exception is technically met with the 50-day average rising — but RSI at 61.8 breaks the extended-ticker gate at Step 2. At a forward price-to-earnings ratio of 25.4x versus the Singapore cluster median of 13.7x (an 85% premium that requires clear justification) and a price-to-growth ratio of 3.68 with revenue growth of just 0.9%, the valuation premium is difficult to defend at current levels. Resistance sits at SGD 5.15; the nearest actionable entry would require a pullback toward SGD 4.80-4.90 with RSI cooling below 55.

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to WATCH — the GTC 2026 keynote on March 16 is the catalyst: per Yahoo Finance reporting, CEO Jensen Huang announced the Blackwell GPU demand remains elevated and offered an anticipated $1 trillion in cumulative revenue through 2027, while also launching a new AI agent platform and a new CPU aimed at Intel's server market. These announcements reframe the near-term narrative from valuation compression to demand confirmation, pulling forward the Blackwell revenue ramp that analysts had been modeling for later quarters. At $183.22, NVDA sits 1.17% below its 50-day average of $185.39 (RSI at 48.8, neutral-bearish) with above-average volume on Monday — the first time volume has confirmed a move in weeks. The 17x forward price-to-earnings ratio with a price-to-growth ratio of 1.11 and 73% revenue growth is a compelling fundamental setup; at in-line pricing versus the semis cluster median of 18.3x forward earnings, there is no valuation overhang at this level. Key support sits at $177.41 and $169.81; near-term resistance at $184.47 and then $194.67. The signal upgrades to BUY on a confirmed close above the 50-day average of $185.39 with volume, ideally with the broader market holding above SPY $665; it reverts to CAUTION on a break below $177.41 support.

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. AMD is 8.9% below its declining 50-day average of $215.77 — not an entry block situation, but the declining average itself is the problem: it signals that the intermediate trend is still deteriorating. RSI at 44.2 is neutral-bearish, and the Helios rack-scale AI platform collaboration with Celestica (announced March 17, per Simply Wall St.) targets late-2026 availability, meaning there is no near-term revenue event to anchor a re-entry. The potentially large Meta GPU supply deal (reportedly MI450-based, per unverified sources that still need SEC filing confirmation) is the multi-quarter thesis, but H2 2026 shipments are too far out to drive a signal change today. At 0.58 price-to-growth ratio with 34% revenue growth, the fundamental case is the strongest on the watchlist — but with a declining 50-day average, above-average extension required for the average to flatten, and AMD CEO Lisa Su reportedly traveling to South Korea to meet Samsung on high-bandwidth memory (per Barchart, March 16), the near-term catalyst queue is thin. AMD becomes interesting as a WATCH candidate if the 50-day average stabilises and price reclaims $200 with volume; the 200-day average at $191.24 is the critical downside reference if support at $196-198 fails.

INTC.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 46x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
INTC.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Signal overridden — the pipeline's soft block flags a forward price-to-earnings ratio of 46x (more than double the semis cluster median of 18.3x) requiring explicit earnings growth justification; with revenue declining 4.1% year-over-year and free cash flow yield at -1.97% (the company is cash flow negative), the growth thesis does not support the multiple at this stage of the turnaround. The Intel 18A node reaching high-volume manufacturing at Fab 52 in Arizona (per a December 2025 report from Financial Content) is the long-duration thesis, but the Panther Lake processor ramp and high-NA EUV customer ramp with Intel are H2 2026 events at the earliest. More immediately negative: Nvidia announced the Groq 3 processor explicitly aimed at Intel's CPU server market during GTC 2026 (per Yahoo Finance, March 16), creating a new competitive pressure vector. At $45.76, Intel sits 0.91% below the 50-day average of $46.18 with RSI at 50 — technically flat, not oversold. Intel reports earnings April 24 (38 days out), which is the next genuine signal catalyst. No entry until the earnings print confirms 18A yield progress and the foundry pipeline materialises into bookings.

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. ASML is essentially sitting on its 50-day average of $1,374.96 (current price $1,375.56, 0.04% above), RSI at 48.8 — as technically neutral as it gets. TD Cowen reiterated a Buy with a $1,500 price target (per Insider Monkey, March 15), and Simply Wall St. reports ASML is expanding into advanced chip packaging through hybrid bonding — a move that extends the company's addressable market beyond its core light-exposure systems into the back-end manufacturing process. At 32x forward price-to-earnings with a 2.3 price-to-growth ratio and just 4.9% revenue growth, the multiple is stretched relative to peers (74.9% premium to the semis cluster median), but the monopoly position in extreme ultraviolet lithography machines — equipment no other company can supply at leading-edge nodes — justifies a premium. Earnings are April 15 (29 days out), a meaningful near-term catalyst. The signal upgrades to WATCH on a pullback toward the 50-day average with higher volume, or on a confirmed break above $1,491 resistance; reverts to CAUTION on a break below the $1,008 support zone.

MU.entry_block: BLOCKED: +13.0% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=6 (<30), RSI 60 (>=55).
MU.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to WATCH (confirmed, but entry remains hard-blocked) — Micron has surged 13% above its 50-day average of $391.00 in just 6 sessions, triggering the extended-ticker hard block. Signal overridden from raw BUY to WATCH — the pipeline blocks entry at +13% above the 50-day average with only 6 sessions above it (minimum 30 required for a trend exception) and RSI at 60.4 (above the 55 threshold). The fundamental momentum is undeniably strong: Micron completed the acquisition of Powerchip Semiconductor's P5 site in Taiwan (per Insider Monkey, March 17) to expand advanced DRAM and high-bandwidth memory capacity for AI data centres, earnings are in 2 days on March 19 (a high-impact binary event), and the 7.8x forward price-to-earnings ratio at a 57% discount to the semis cluster median makes this one of the most attractively valued names on the watchlist. Micron's CEO has projected memory prices to rise approximately 40% through Q2 2026 — though this is a bullish call from a seller and should be treated with appropriate scepticism pending independent corroboration. Do not enter ahead of Thursday's earnings print; a beat with strong high-bandwidth memory guidance would be the catalyst to reassess. If post-earnings the stock pulls back toward $400-410 while the 50-day average continues rising, that is the entry zone worth targeting.

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Amazon is 5% below its declining 50-day average of $222.92, which in isolation looks like an entry zone — but the declining average itself signals the intermediate trend has not stabilised. RSI at 47.1, neutral-bearish. The EU GDPR fine appeal win (€746 million fine removed, per Simply Wall St., March 16) is a genuine positive and reduces a legal overhang in Europe, while the expansion of AI-enabled healthcare and pharmacy offerings adds a new growth vector. However, the $200 billion AI capital expenditure cycle is simultaneously compressing near-term earnings — the company's own forecast for Q1 operating income disappointed when announced, and free cash flow yield of just 1.05% means the thesis is entirely dependent on future AWS acceleration materialising. Amazon reports earnings May 1 (45 days out). At $211.74 with support at $211.22 (effectively the current price — a thin margin), a break below that level opens a test of $196.93. Would reconsider to WATCH on a confirmed close above the 50-day average of $222.92.

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Alphabet sits 4.52% below its declining 50-day average of $318.83 with RSI at 44.2 — technically weak, not yet oversold. The Google Cloud AI narrative is intact: per Simply Wall St. (March 17), Alphabet announced a $175-185 billion capital plan for 2026 centered on AI infrastructure, and Google Cloud partnerships with TPU v7 deployments for Meta and Anthropic confirm the hardware strategy is gaining traction. Per Reuters (March 17), Google is also in talks with Chinese liquid cooling equipment suppliers for data centres, reflecting tight supply of advanced cooling systems globally. However, the 2.29 price-to-growth ratio with 18% revenue growth at a 22.7x forward price-to-earnings ratio is in-line with the BigTech cluster median — no discount to justify entering against a declining trend. Earnings are April 24 (38 days out). Support at $295.95 is the next level to watch; a hold there with volume improvement would be the first sign of stabilisation. Would reconsider to WATCH on a close back above the $319 resistance zone.

LITE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +21.9% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.
LITE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Signal overridden from raw CAUTION to CAUTION (confirmed) — LITE is 21.9% above its 50-day average of $512.50, the extreme extension zone with no exceptions permitted regardless of trend duration. Per 24/7 Wall St. (March 16), Lumentum rallied on the back of Nebius announcing a five-year agreement with Meta valued at up to $27 billion (a framework ceiling, not a firm contract), which investors read as confirmation of the AI data centre buildout thesis. The live Marvell-Lumentum optical circuit switching demo at OFC 2026 (per Simply Wall St., March 16) is a genuine product development signal. The reported NVIDIA $2 billion strategic investment into Lumentum remains unverified — it requires an SEC 8-K filing confirmation before treating as fact. At a 0.13 price-to-growth ratio despite a 43.1x forward price-to-earnings ratio (because earnings growth expectations are extraordinary), the valuation math is growth-dependent and fragile. LITE is not an entry at current levels; the nearest meaningful support is $528 (a prior high). Only interesting below $560, and then only with confirmation that the broader AI capex cycle is re-accelerating.

COHR.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — COHR is now 10.51% above its 50-day average of $223.84 and the extended-ticker decision tree blocks entry at Step 2: RSI at 51.6 is below the 55 threshold, so the block would not apply on RSI grounds, but the 10.5% extension exceeds the 5% hard-block threshold and the trend has only been in place for 132 sessions. Working through the decision tree: RSI 51.6 passes Step 2; 132 sessions above the 50-day average passes Step 3; the 50-day average is rising (passes Step 4); Step 5 requires a structural multi-quarter demand driver — the S&P 500 inclusion (March 9) and the reported (unverified) NVIDIA investment are partially structural, but the inclusion catalyst has already been exhausted per the photonics cluster narrative, and the NVIDIA investment lacks 8-K confirmation. The decision tree lands at BLOCKED — the driver has not definitively graduated from inclusion-spike to structural demand. At 0.05 price-to-growth ratio with 73% earnings growth forecast, fundamentals are attractive for patient entry. A pullback to $223-228 (the rising 50-day average zone) would represent the proper entry; the $300 resistance is the upside target if the broader AI data centre thesis holds.

XLE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +11.0% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: RSI 71 (>=55).
XLE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Signal overridden from raw HOLD to CAUTION — XLE is 11% above its 50-day average of $52.16 with RSI at 71.1, firmly in overbought territory, triggering the hard block. The energy ETF has gained 7.26% in a month on the back of the Hormuz oil shock, which is now in its eighth day. Per Bloomberg (via macro_news), Brent crude posted its biggest one-day gain since June 2025 as Hormuz shipping ground to a near-halt; Citigroup analysts projected a $80-90 per barrel base case range for the near term. The geopolitical supply premium is real, but so is the demand destruction risk — at $97.71 per barrel, WTI is 19% above recent lows but still 13% below pre-crisis highs per the news digest, reflecting market uncertainty about duration. As an ETF, XLE has no valuation metrics to anchor a fundamental call; the technical story is the whole story, and the story is overbought with no clean entry. Only interesting on a pullback to $53-54, approximately aligned with the 50-day average, if the Hormuz situation stabilises rather than escalates further.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 82x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Palantir's AI Platform for commercial customers and its defence AI wins remain the thesis — the $10 billion Army framework ceiling (an indefinite-delivery contract, not firm revenue) and the $448 million Navy ShipOS deal (per DoD press release) underpin the defence narrative, and Wedbush analysts maintained their Outperform view citing rising enterprise and government AI platform demand (per Proactive, March 16). The problem is entirely valuation: at 81.2x forward price-to-earnings — a 260% premium to the BigTech cluster median — the pipeline's soft block requires explicit earnings growth justification. Palantir's own forecast for 2026 revenue of $7.18-7.20 billion implies 61% year-over-year growth, and the 3.22 price-to-growth ratio is expensive. A Motley Fool piece (March 17) argues Alphabet will outperform Palantir over five years — illustrating the valuation debate is live and unresolved. At $152.72, just 0.41% below the 50-day average of $153.35, the stock is in no-man's land technically (RSI 55, neutral-bullish). Upgrades to WATCH on a pullback to $140-145 support range with RSI cooling below 48; the $187-190 resistance zone is the ceiling absent a valuation re-rating.

VUAA_L.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Would reconsider above USD 132.31 (the declining 50-day average) with sustained volume — the 200-day average at USD 126.85 is the downside reference if current levels fail.

singapore.summary: The Singapore cluster remains uniformly cautious for a seventh consecutive session, with the banking trio (DBS, OCBC, UOB) all below declining or flat 50-day averages and Singapore Airlines now downgraded to CAUTION as Hormuz-driven jet fuel inflation directly pressures its cost base. The divergence worth watching: Singtel (Z74_SI) is the only name technically extended to the upside, but the 85% forward price-to-earnings premium to the cluster median and a hard entry block make it an observer, not a participant.
singapore.thesis_status: Structural weakness — no recovery catalyst until US10Y breaks below 4.20%
singapore.key_development: SIA downgraded to CAUTION as Middle East airspace closures and jet fuel cost surge compound the banking sector's net interest margin compression into a cluster-wide headwind.

semiconductors.summary: GTC 2026 is reshaping the semis cluster in real time: NVIDIA's upgrade to WATCH on AI demand confirmation diverges sharply from AMD's and Intel's ongoing CAUTION signals, reinforcing the thesis that this is a selective, not broad, semis recovery. Micron's earnings in 48 hours (March 19) is the cluster's next binary event — a beat with strong high-bandwidth memory guidance would validate the mid-cap foundry thesis and potentially catalyse an upgrade from hard-blocked WATCH to actionable entry post-pullback.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Selective recovery — NVDA leading, AMD/INTC lagging, MU the near-term pivot point
semiconductors.key_development: Nvidia's GTC keynote projecting $1 trillion cumulative revenue through 2027 and announcing a CPU challenger to Intel signals the company is expanding its competitive surface area while core Blackwell GPU demand remains elevated.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is the weakest cluster in Monday's rally — AMZN and GOOG both remain below declining 50-day averages despite meaningful AI capex narratives, while Palantir holds at HOLD on valuation grounds. The divergence within the cluster is notable: Palantir's defence AI wins are driving real government contract flow, while Amazon's and Alphabet's AI spend is front-loaded capital expenditure competing with each other for the same hyperscaler infrastructure.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structural underperformance — no entry signals across the cluster
big_tech.key_development: Alphabet's $175-185 billion 2026 AI capital plan (per Simply Wall St.) and Google's procurement talks with Chinese cooling suppliers (per Reuters) confirm the infrastructure arms race is accelerating, but near-term earnings compression from this spend keeps the signal at CAUTION.

photonics.summary: The photonics cluster is sending conflicting signals: LITE has been hard-blocked at extreme extension for multiple sessions as the S&P 500 inclusion catalyst exhausts, while COHR has now been downgraded from WATCH to CAUTION as its 10.5% extension above the 50-day average no longer justifies entry after the inclusion spike. The OFC 2026 Marvell-Lumentum demo (per Simply Wall St.) and the Meta-Nebius AI infrastructure deal providing a read-through to optical demand keep the long-term thesis intact — but both names need to pull back materially before a clean entry exists.
photonics.thesis_status: Thesis intact, technically exhausted — patient entry only
photonics.key_development: COHR downgraded from WATCH to CAUTION as the post-inclusion extension exceeds the 5% hard-block threshold without a verified structural catalyst (the reported NVIDIA $2 billion investment remains unconfirmed pending SEC filing).

energy.summary: XLE is technically overbought at 71.1 RSI and 11% above its 50-day average — a pure geopolitical-driven spike with no clean entry. The Hormuz crisis narrative (vessel traffic at 2 per day versus a 138 baseline, per the news digest) is providing the supply shock, but Citigroup's base case of $80-90 per barrel Brent (per Bloomberg macro_news) implies the market is already pricing significant disruption. Commodity-signal follow-through note: with XLE already 11% extended, no attractive entry exists — the signal is to avoid and monitor for a pullback toward $53-54.
energy.thesis_status: Overbought — geopolitical premium elevated, no entry
energy.key_development: WTI at $97.71 continues to reflect the Hormuz supply shock, but the data conflict between fast_info and history pricing (+1.8% vs +4.5%) warrants caution in interpreting the single-day move — the 5-day trend of +2.81% for XLE is a more reliable measure of the underlying pressure.

index.summary: VUAA.L (which tracks the S&P 500) remains on CAUTION with the ETF 2.7% below its declining 50-day average and RSI at 40.3 — technically the weakest broad index signal on the watchlist. Monday's SPY recovery to $669 helps, but VUAA.L's 200-day average at $126.85 is just 1.5% below current price, meaning the ETF is close to a major structural support test.
index.thesis_status: Cautious — below declining 50-day average, 200-day average support nearby
index.key_development: VIX's 13.6% single-day compression to 23.51 reduces immediate tail risk, but the FOMC decision on Wednesday is the next test of whether Monday's relief rally has structural follow-through.

interconnected.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (US ADR)
interconnected.reason: Manufactures Blackwell and future Vera Rubin GPUs for NVDA — the GTC 2026 demand signal directly translates into utilisation at TSMC's advanced nodes, and Micron's new Taiwan P5 fab acquisition adds context for the island's semiconductor capacity expansion.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if TSM pulls back 8-10% from current levels to align with a potential broader semis consolidation post-Micron earnings on March 19.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix
interconnected.reason: The leading high-bandwidth memory supplier for NVDA's Blackwell architecture — Micron's (MU) aggressive Taiwan capacity expansion to compete in the same market makes SK Hynix's pricing and margin trajectory the most direct read-through to MU's earnings thesis.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor after MU earnings on March 19 for confirmation that high-bandwidth memory pricing is rising as MU's CEO projects — if MU beats on memory pricing, SK Hynix becomes a watchlist candidate at any pullback of 5%+ from current Korean won levels.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Directly interconnected to the LITE/COHR photonics thesis — the live OFC 2026 rack-level demo of Marvell optical connectivity interoperating with Lumentum's optical circuit switching platform (per Simply Wall St.) shows Marvell as the networking silicon layer in the same AI data centre buildout that drives both photonics names.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Marvell pulls back 10% or more from current levels, creating alignment with a potential photonics cluster consolidation after the post-inclusion extension exhausts.

interconnected.name: Broadcom
interconnected.reason: Custom AI chip developer for hyperscalers (Google, Meta) — the same $175-185 billion Alphabet AI capital plan that keeps GOOG on CAUTION is Broadcom's revenue opportunity, as custom application-specific chips for Google's AI accelerators represent multi-year contracted revenue.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after Alphabet's April 24 earnings for confirmation that TPU v7 and custom silicon deployment rates are accelerating, which would validate the Broadcom hyperscaler custom chip thesis.

interconnected.name: SPDR Gold Shares ETF
interconnected.reason: Gold at $5,014 reflects persistent safe-haven demand from the Hormuz crisis and sticky inflation — with the FOMC decision on March 19 potentially reinforcing the rate-pause narrative, gold's role as an inflation and geopolitical hedge remains directly relevant to the portfolio's risk posture.
interconnected.entry_note: Already extended — only interesting below $4,850, approximately a 3% pullback from current levels, which would align with a post-FOMC dovish surprise reducing the safe-haven premium slightly without breaking the structural bid.

active_risks: Iran-US/Israel conflict threatening Strait of Hormuz oil and LNG shipments — vessel traffic at ~2 per day vs 138 baseline
US-China tech export controls and 100% tariffs reshaping semiconductor supply chain and constraining ASML, NVDA, AMD China revenue
Fed hawkish rate pause compressing tech multiples as sticky inflation delays rate cuts
geopolitical.macro_outlook: The dominant transmission channel right now is the oil supply shock from the Hormuz disruption — roughly 20% of the world's daily oil supply passes through the strait, and with vessel traffic effectively halted, the secondary effects (jet fuel costs for airlines, freight inflation for logistics, energy margin expansion for XLE, demand destruction risk for global growth) are compressing margin guidance across multiple clusters simultaneously. The hawkish Fed pause narrative adds a second layer: per the December 2025 FOMC projections, the median dot for 2026 rates remains elevated, and February CPI at 0.3% month-over-month (released March 12) has pushed rate-cut expectations further out. These two forces — supply shock inflation and a Fed unwilling to ease into it — create the most challenging environment for growth equity valuations in the portfolio.

base_case.description: The Hormuz disruption persists through April but partial rerouting via secondary routes (Iranian exports to China, Omani alternative corridors) contains the worst-case supply collapse. WTI stabilises in the $90-105 range. The Fed holds rates at the March 19 FOMC meeting and signals two cuts in H2 2026, consistent with the December dot plot median. Tech multiples stabilise but do not re-expand. VIX holds in the 20-25 range.
base_case.market_impact: SPY consolidates in the $655-680 range. XLE remains elevated but not overbought. Semis selective recovery continues with NVDA and MU as the primary beneficiaries. Singapore banks remain under pressure until rate cut expectations firm.
base_case.portfolio_action: Hold current WATCH stance on NVDA and COHR pending pullbacks. Await MU earnings on March 19 before adding the semis thesis. No new broad index exposure via VUAA.L until SPY confirms above $680.

optimistic_case.description: A US-Iran diplomatic channel opens within two weeks, Hormuz shipping resumes partial operations, and WTI drops back toward $85. The FOMC on March 19 signals a more dovish path than expected — potentially one cut in Q2 — driven by growth concerns from the oil shock. VIX falls below 18.
optimistic_case.market_impact: SPY could recover to $690-700. BigTech (AMZN, GOOG) lifts off declining 50-day averages and approaches WATCH territory. XLE gives back some gains. Singapore banks re-rate on improved carry-trade economics.
optimistic_case.portfolio_action: Upgrade VUAA.L to WATCH if SPY breaks above $680 with volume. Revisit GOOG and AMZN entries if their 50-day averages flatten. Reduce urgency on energy exposure — XLE becomes less attractive as geopolitical premium deflates.

pessimistic_case.description: The Hormuz conflict escalates materially — Iran formally closes the strait, Kuwait and UAE begin curtailing production (as JPMorgan analysts warned in the macro_news, 18-22 days of storage buffer remaining), and WTI surges toward $115-120 per barrel. The Fed holds firmly with no rate cut signal at the March 19 FOMC. Corporate Q1 guidance warnings on fuel and freight costs begin emerging.
pessimistic_case.market_impact: SPY tests $640-650 support. VIX spikes back above 30. Mega-cap tech (AMZN, GOOG, NVDA) faces renewed multiple compression. XLE benefits short-term but demand destruction eventually caps gains above $120 Brent. Singapore Airlines (C6L_SI) faces acute earnings risk.
pessimistic_case.portfolio_action: Pause all new entries across the watchlist. If SPY breaks $650, consider GLD as a portfolio hedge. Avoid adding to C6L_SI — fuel cost guidance could trigger an additional downgrade. Monitor WTI $105 as the level where demand destruction begins to fight the supply shock.

wildcard.description: US-China tech trade war escalates further — the administration expands semiconductor export controls beyond the current entity list to cover additional advanced chip architectures (similar to the October 2022 and October 2023 rule expansions), forcing NVDA, AMD, and ASML to revise China revenue guidance downward materially mid-quarter.
wildcard.market_impact: Semis cluster faces a gap-down event. NVDA could retest $169-177 support. ASML faces China revenue risk (approximately 30-35% of sales historically). AMD and INTC face mixed effects — short-term disruption but long-term US foundry thesis strengthens.
wildcard.portfolio_action: Singapore names and extended positions like LITE and COHR face sharpest forced selling in a broad tech risk-off. Do not add to any semis position ahead of anticipated export control announcements — hold cash and wait for post-announcement stabilisation before re-entering NVDA near $177 support.

events_this_week.event: WTI crude at $97.71 — Strait of Hormuz vessel traffic at approximately 2 ships per day versus a baseline of 138; supply disruption risk is live
Gold at $5,014 — above $5,000 level reflects persistent safe-haven demand from Hormuz conflict and sticky inflation
VIX at 23.51 — fell 13.6% on Monday; fear has compressed but remains elevated above the 20 threshold that typically signals stable market conditions
SPY at $669.03 — testing recovery above the $665 support band; breadth conviction still thin with mega-cap tech underperforming

FOMC Rate Decision (14:00 ET) — the Federal Reserve announces its interest rate decision; market expects a hold, with the press conference providing the key signal on whether two 2026 cuts remain on the table

Micron (MU) Earnings — binary event with MU having surged 13% above its 50-day average ahead of the print; a beat with strong high-bandwidth memory guidance could catalyse the semis cluster, while a miss would likely trigger a sharp pullback
ASML Earnings (approximate, 29 days out) — key read-through on extreme ultraviolet lithography order book, China revenue under export controls, and High-NA EUV ramp timeline with Intel

Alphabet (GOOG) Earnings (approximate, 38 days out) — Google Cloud growth rate and AI capital expenditure pace are the two numbers that matter; any guidance on TPU v7 deployment revenue would be a signal catalyst
Intel (INTC) Earnings (approximate, 38 days out) — 18A node yield progress, Panther Lake ramp timeline, and foundry customer additions are the thesis tests for a potential signal upgrade
DBS Group (D05_SI) Earnings (approximate, 44 days out) — net interest margin trajectory and wealth management fee income are the two metrics that would shift the Singapore banking cluster signal

Blackwell Ultra (B300) volume production ramp — per NVDA Q4 FY2026 earnings guidance, next-generation GPU architecture entering high-volume manufacturing; the pace of this ramp determines whether NVDA's $78 billion Q1 FY2027 guidance is achievable

AMD Helios AI platform availability — Helios rack-scale AI platform (AMD Instinct accelerators + open networking) targeting cloud, enterprise, and research customers in late 2026; first revenue confirmation of AMD's hyperscaler AI strategy

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive with selective Watch positions in semis
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: The simultaneous pressure of the Hormuz oil supply shock (raising costs across airlines, logistics, and consumer staples) and the Fed's hawkish rate pause (compressing tech multiples) creates a dual headwind that keeps 11 of 17 tickers at CAUTION — the dominant near-term risk is that the FOMC on March 19 reinforces the no-cut narrative while MU's earnings add binary volatility to the semis cluster.

watch_for_entry.note: Signal upgraded to WATCH on GTC 2026 demand confirmation. Actionable on a confirmed close above the 50-day average of $185.39 with above-average volume — upgrades to BUY candidate at that trigger.

watch_for_entry.note: OCBC sitting just above its 50-day average at SGD 20.89 — needs a second confirmed close above with volume. Closest to a clean entry among Singapore names. Half position if triggered.

on_deck.note: 0.58 price-to-growth ratio with 34% revenue growth — best fundamental setup on the watchlist. Technically blocked by a declining 50-day average. Earnings not until May 6.

on_deck.trigger: 50-day average of $215.77 stabilises (flattens or turns rising) AND price reclaims $200 on above-average volume over two sessions

on_deck.note: Downgraded to CAUTION but fundamentally attractive — 73% earnings growth forecast and 0.05 price-to-growth ratio. Extended 10.5% above 50-day average; needs to cool.
on_deck.rationale: Free cash flow yield of 0.28% is low, but 17.5% revenue growth and extraordinary earnings growth trajectory at a 12.2% discount to the photonics cluster median price-to-earnings makes this the cheaper of the two photonics names once technically accessible.
on_deck.trigger: Pullback to $225-235 range (50-day average zone) with RSI cooling below 48 and SPY holding above $660

Avoid. Negative free cash flow yield, declining revenue, and a 152% price-to-earnings premium to the semis cluster median — the turnaround is real but not yet in the financials.
Avoid at current levels. Free cash flow yield of 1.05% and a declining 50-day average means the $200 billion AI capital expenditure is consuming cash faster than AWS is generating it near-term.
Avoid. No technical reason to enter against a declining 50-day average; the AI infrastructure story is intact but not discounted at 22.7x forward earnings.
Avoid. 21.9% extreme extension with the reported NVIDIA investment still unverified — no entry exists at current levels regardless of the long-term thesis.
Avoid. RSI at 71.1 and 11% above the 50-day average — overbought on a geopolitical spike. Revisit on a pullback to the $53-54 range.
Avoid. S&P 500 index exposure below its declining 50-day average — wait for FOMC outcome and MU earnings before reassessing broad index entry.
Avoid. UOB below declining 50-day average with net interest margin compression ongoing; no catalyst to separate it from the broader Singapore banking weakness.

hold_no_action.note: Signal unchanged at HOLD. DBS at SGD 56.89 — 1.2% below 50-day average with RSI 52.8. Upgrades to WATCH on a close above SGD 57.59 with volume.

Signal unchanged at HOLD. ASML sitting exactly on its 50-day average — no entry dislocation. Upgrades to WATCH on a volume-confirmed pullback to $1,350-1,360 or a break above $1,491 resistance. Earnings April 15.

hold_no_action.note: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Valuation at 81.2x forward earnings blocks entry despite strong defence AI contract momentum. Upgrades to WATCH on a pullback to $140-145 with RSI below 48.

bullish_upgrades: MU → entry becomes actionable on post-earnings pullback toward $400-415 once the 30-session trend exception clock resets

ASML → WATCH candidate as memory capex cycle sustains equipment orders
